title: Bitter Crispy American Cheddar Salad
description: A bold, keto-friendly salad that pairs bitter vine tomatoes with crunchy, crumbly cheddar and fresh greens, finished with a zesty lemonolive oil dressing.

ingredients.name: vine tomatoes
ingredients.notes: preferably slightly bitter, like heirloom or plum varieties

ingredients.name: mixed salad greens
ingredients.notes: arugula or mizuna for added pepperiness

ingredients.name: panko breadcrumbs
ingredients.notes: kept keto‑friendly by using low‑carb panko if available

ingredients.name: olive oil
ingredients.notes: for baking the cheese and dressing

instructions: In a shallow dish, combine the panko breadcrumbs, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper.
Pat the cheddar cubes dry and coat each cube evenly in the breadcrumb mixture.
Place the coated cheese on a parchment‑lined baking sheet and drizzle with 1 tablespoon olive oil.
Bake for 12–15 minutes, or until the cheese is golden and crunchy. Remove and let it cool for 5 minutes; this is the "rest" step.
Meanwhile, toss the chopped vine tomatoes and mixed greens in a large bowl with the remaining tablespoon of olive oil and fresh lemon juice.
Add the freshly baked cheddar cubes to the salad and gently toss to combine.
Sprinkle chopped parsley over the top for a burst of color and herbaceous flavor.
Serve immediately while the cheese is still crisp, or chill for up to 30 minutes in the refrigerator.
